bug_report.summary: Tab-strip control near “Split View” tab (close/indicator area) renders differently after change (possible misrender/hover/clip)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the app to the main interface (as in the scenario, after skipping the setup wizard).
Observe the tab strip at the top of the editor, specifically the right side of the “Split View” tab (close/controls region).
expected_behaviors: Tab close/control area should render consistently and correctly (icon/shape/position not changing unexpectedly between builds).
actual_behaviors: A small visual change is present in the “Split View” tab’s close/controls region (bbox ~[749,171]-[772,188]), indicating the control/icon/state is rendered differently after the code change. This appears unrelated to the intended table editor UX improvements.

bug_report.summary: Visual artifact/misalignment near the “Helpful Links” heading marker (#) in the right editor pane
steps_to_reproduce: Open the tutorial content so the “Helpful Links” heading is visible in the lower-right pane.
Observe the left side of the heading where the green “#” heading marker is shown.
expected_behaviors: Heading marker and its left margin/decorations should render cleanly without stray bars/overlaps, and be consistent between builds.
actual_behaviors: A small rendering difference is present near the “Helpful Links” heading marker/margin (diff bbox ~[895,538]-[905,584]), suggesting an added/shifted vertical bar or slight misalignment around the heading indicator between before/after builds.

bug_report.summary: Backspace at beginning of table cell moves caret unexpectedly (caret position jumps)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file.
Insert a pipe table with 2 columns and 3 total rows (header + 2 body rows).
Click in the first body cell (row 1, col 1) and type “alpha”.
Place the caret immediately before the “a” in “alpha”.
Press Backspace once.
expected_behaviors: Backspace at the start of a table cell should not move the caret to a different position (and should not cross/delete table boundaries).
Caret position indicators (line/column) should remain consistent with being at the start of the cell content.
actual_behaviors: After the code change, the caret position indicators change (e.g., from ~4:3 to ~4:9 as shown in the top-right/status bar), indicating the caret jumped to a different location even though the cell text remains “alpha”.

bug_report.summary: Multiline paste sanitization drops an entire line of text instead of replacing newline with a space
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Insert a 2-column, 4-row table (1 header + 3 body).
Click into the first body cell (row 1, col 1).

Paste multiline text:
First line of pasted text.
Second line should become a space.
Third line ends here.
Observe the resulting cell content.
expected_behaviors: All pasted text remains present, with line breaks sanitized (e.g., converted into spaces) so content becomes a single-line string in the cell: “First line of pasted text. Second line should become a space. Third line ends here.”
actual_behaviors: The middle line text (“Second line should become a space.”) is missing after paste; only the first and third lines remain visible in the cell (diff region 3 ~[898,515]-[1164,733]).

bug_report.summary: Typing below a table may still insert text into a table cell instead of normal document text (cursor fails to leave table)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Insert a table with 2 columns and 4 total rows (1 header + 3 body).
Click into a table cell.
Click below the table (normal document text area) and type: "below".
Click back into the original cell to inspect surrounding content/state.
expected_behaviors: After clicking below the table, the caret moves outside the table and typed text (“below”) appears in normal document text, not inside any table cell.
actual_behaviors: In the after-change screenshot, the word “below” is visible inside the table grid (diff region 4 ~[923,700]-[978,721]), indicating text entry may remain within the table instead of below it.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: Malformed pipe table incorrectly renders as a formatted table in preview after editing (data/structure loss)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Paste the malformed pipe table:
| A | B |
|---|---|
| 1 | 2 | 3 |
| 4 |
Append " X" to the end of the last line so it becomes "| 4 | X".
Observe the preview pane rendering of this table block.
expected_behaviors: Because the table remains malformed (a data row has 3 cells while the header has 2), it should stay as raw markdown text in the preview (not be converted into a rendered table).
actual_behaviors: The preview renders the malformed pipe table as a formatted table (with table UI controls/handles) and appears to drop/truncate the extra cell from the "| 1 | 2 | 3 |" row.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: Grid-table markdown gets inserted into existing pipe-table cell instead of becoming a new raw block below (table boundary corruption)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Paste the malformed pipe table text.
Edit the last line to add " X" (so the last line becomes "| 4 | X").
Attempt to add a new line below the table and paste a grid table markdown block starting with "+-----+-----+".
Observe where the pasted grid-table text ends up.
expected_behaviors: Grid-table markdown should appear as its own raw text block below the pipe table (since grid tables are unsupported for rendering in this scenario), and should not be inserted into/modify an existing pipe table cell.
actual_behaviors: The grid-table markdown appears inside the existing table cell after "X" (e.g., the cell shows "X +-----+-----+"), indicating the editor did not leave the table context and the paste corrupted the table boundary.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: After clicking “+ column”, the horizontal bar/overlay beneath the table renders inconsistently (filled gray vs empty/outlined)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Insert a 2-column pipe table with a header row and 2 body rows.
Click into the first body cell and enter some text (e.g., “kiwi”).
Click the “+ column” control above the table headers to add a column.
expected_behaviors: Adding a column should only change the table structure and controls; no unexpected overlay/blank bar should appear below the table, and any table-editor UI elements should render consistently.
actual_behaviors: A wide horizontal bar/overlay area below the table changes appearance after the code change (diff at bbox ~[894,667]-[1223,690]): before shows a filled gray bar, after shows an empty/outlined bar, suggesting a rendering/style or focus-state issue triggered by adding a column.
